title: Advertiser Solutions Vendor Lead - Programmatic and Direct Support
description:
Support what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Advertiser Solutions team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them through best in class service; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 

We are looking for a Vendor Lead to serve as the primary connection between our vendor support teams and the broader Spotify Ads organization. You'll help drive operational excellence across EMEA and JAPAC markets, ensuring our advertiser support experience is consistent, scalable, and high quality. Working across vendor partners and internal stakeholders, you'll identify opportunities for improvement, lead operational initiatives, and help shape how frontline support is delivered across our advertising business.
department: Advertising Sales - Emerging and Scaled Sales

title: Analytics Engineer II
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

We’re looking for an Analytics Engineer II to join Spotify's Platform Central Data (PCD) squad, a cross-functional Data Engineering and Analytics Engineering team within the Platform Mission. You’ll help build and maintain trusted analytical models, metrics, and data products that power developer productivity, platform health, and leadership decision-making. Working closely with Data Engineers, Product, Engineering, and Platform partners, you’ll translate platform signals into reliable, well-modeled data assets that help Spotify ship faster and safer.

title: Artist Label Partnerships Manager, Taiwan
description:
We are looking for a strategic-minded, execution-oriented Artists & Label Partnerships Manager for Taiwan, to join Spotify’s Music team based in Taipei. In this role, you will be part of the Music team in Southeast Asia, reporting to the regional Head of Artists & Label Partnerships.
 
You will sit at the heart of the music ecosystem, moving between high-level release strategies and the day-to-day tactical execution that drives artist and business growth. This role is perfect for a music industry professional who thrives on building relationships and navigating the intersection of technology and creativity.
 
Our ideal candidate has fun at work and a desire to make a positive impact on the music industry as a whole. Above all, your work will impact the way the world experiences music and strengthen Spotify’s role as a trusted, culturally relevant voice in the music landscape. This role is based in Taipei and will require international travel occasionally.

title: Assistant Manager, Music Marketing - South Korea (Contractor)
description:
Spotify is seeking an Assistant Manager, Music Marketing in South Korea to lead impactful music marketing campaigns across Korea’s dynamic music landscape. As part of the Korea Music Marketing team, you will define marketing strategies and deliver outstanding campaigns that connect artists and fans through music, culture, live experiences, and storytelling. You will execute music release campaigns end-to-end, contributing to audience engagement, brand affinity, cultural relevance, and subscriber growth in Korea. Most importantly, you have fun at work and a desire to make a positive impact for music fans and audio listeners globally.

This is a 8-month contract role reporting directly to the Head of Kpop Marketing.

title: Associate Client Partner - Emerging & Scaled (New Business, German Speaking)
description:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 

The Emerging & Scaled team is focused on unlocking growth from new and growing advertisers across Spotify’s ads business. The New Business team brings first-time advertisers onto the platform and builds the foundation for long-term partnerships. Germany is a key market within our Northern Europe E&S organization. Based in London, you will partner closely with our existing German Client Partners and Sales leadership to build a dedicated New Business pipeline and expand Spotify’s advertiser base across Germany.

This role focuses on new business acquisition. You will source, qualify, and close net-new advertisers, building relationships from scratch and converting first-time buyers into long-term Spotify partners. Reporting to the Head of E&S Europe, you will be part of a dedicated cross-market New Business team focused on net-new advertiser acquisition and revenue growth across Europe.

You’re motivated, proactive, and energized by building new relationships.. Integrity and a healthy work-life balance are non-negotiable. You’ll shape how new advertisers discover Spotify and elevate their connection to our platform.
department: Advertising Sales - Emerging and Scaled Sales

Based in Sydney, you will partner closely with our existing ANZ Client Partners and Sales leadership to build a dedicated New Business pipeline and expand Spotify’s advertiser base across Australia and New Zealand.

This role focuses on new business acquisition. You will source, qualify, and close net-new advertisers, building relationships from scratch and converting first-time buyers into long-term Spotify partners. You’re motivated, proactive, and energized by building new relationships.. Integrity and a healthy work-life balance are non-negotiable. You’ll shape how new advertisers discover Spotify and elevate their connection to our platform.

title: Backend Engineer - Platform
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
 
Starfleet sits at the heart of Spotify’s developer experience evolution. We build and operate critical internal platforms like Fleetshift — the engine powering large-scale automated code refactoring across Spotify — and Production Policy Enforcer, which secures and streamlines our production fleet. Now, we’re leading one of the most transformative shifts in Spotify’s engineering culture: migrating from a polyrepo ecosystem to a unified monorepo model.
 
This is not just a tooling migration. It’s a rethinking of how thousands of engineers build, test, and ship software every day. As part of Starfleet, you’ll help shape the foundation of Spotify’s engineering future.

title: Business Development Senior Manager, APAC
description:
At Spotify, we’re passionate about delivering the right audio experience for every moment. We’ve connected hundreds of millions of people to the content they love and built the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service. We operate in more than 180 markets and continue to scale rapidly across APAC.
 
We’re building a world-class business development team and are looking for a multi-talented self-starter with ambition and drive who will help grow Spotify’s Premium distribution partnerships in the APAC region. You will own, negotiate, and scale strategic agreements with partners who can accelerate subscriber and engagement growth. This role will be based in either Singapore or Seoul and you will report to the APAC Director of Business Development. Above all, your work will impact the way the world experiences audio.

title: C++ Engineer - Platform Engineering
description:
The CI Experience squad owns the client continuous integration (CI) infrastructure at Spotify. Our mission is to ensure smooth, reliable, and fast integration of code changes across all client repositories. We provide the tooling, documentation, and support that empower client engineers to build, test, and ship confidently.
 
We’re evolving our CI platform to meet the growing complexity of Spotify’s client ecosystem. Spanning Android, iOS, desktop, and embedded platforms, by building scalable, maintainable systems and best practices for continuous delivery.

title: Client Partner - Emerging & Scaled (Nordics / UK)
description:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 

We are looking for an exceptional Client Partner to join our Emerging & Scaled team, one of the fastest-growing units within Spotify’s Ad Sales organization. This role is perfect for someone who wakes up every morning excited to connect with clients and inspire them with the power of Spotify. 

You bring curiosity, resilience, and a collaborative mindset, along with a passion for building meaningful client partnerships. Integrity and a healthy work-life balance are non-negotiable. You’ll shape how Spotify advertisers reach audiences and elevate their connection to our platform.
department: Advertising Sales - Emerging and Scaled Sales

title: Consumer Marketing Manager, East Africa
description:
Spotify’s Marketing team is focused on connecting artists and creators with fans in ways that feel authentic, culturally relevant, and impactful. As part of the broader Markets & Subscriber Growth organization, this team helps grow Spotify’s presence across emerging and high-growth markets by developing locally resonant campaigns that drive acquisition, engagement, and retention. In this role, you’ll work across East Africa and selected Sub-Saharan African markets, partnering closely with global and regional teams to shape how Spotify shows up for listeners every day.
 
This position is available through a third party as a Long-Term Contractor.

title: Creative Business Strategies (CBS) Manager - Colombia
description:
Creative Business Strategies (CBS) is Spotify LATAM’s cultural innovation hub. We design and execute cross-functional cultural initiatives that strengthen Spotify’s leadership in Music Culture and power the Creator Flywheel across the region.
 
A core pillar of this role is leading the operation and coordination of the programming at Casa Spotify as a cultural infrastructure in the market. Casa is a permanent, living platform where artists, creators, fans and industry connect through curated experiences that support Spotify’s cultural relevance and business priorities.
 
The CBS Manager works at the intersection of Music, Marketing, Podcast, PR/Comms, Ads and Operations to ensure initiatives are strategically aligned, sustainably executed and meaningfully integrated within Casa Spotify. Content strategy and artist priorities are defined by the respective teams; CBS ensures orchestration, feasibility and operational excellence.
 
This position reports to the Head of CBS LATAM and collaborates closely with local vertical leads as well as the broader CBS regional team.

title: Creative Business Strategies (CBS) Manager - São Paulo (Contractor)
description:
Spotify's Creative Business Strategies (CBS) team develops innovative experiences that deepen fan engagement and create meaningful connections between audiences, creators, and culture. As part of CBS LATAM, this engagement will support a major live experience project led by the team in Brazil, working across talent, content, and cross-functional coordination.
 

Contract Duration: June 2026 - February 2027

title: Data Engineer – Platform
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.


The Data Infrastructure product area sits at the heart of Spotify’s Platform mission, enabling teams across the company to solve complex data challenges at scale. We build the infrastructure and tooling that powers data pipelines used in some of Spotify’s most loved experiences, like Discover Weekly and Wrapped. As part of this team, you’ll collaborate with engineers across multiple squads to shape the future of data processing and developer experience at Spotify, especially as AI and intelligent agents become more central to how we work with data.

title: Data Scientist II - Platform Mission
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
 
The SAFE Insights team supports the five studios that make up the SAFE Alliance: User Platform, User Fraud, IT, Security, and PACE (Procurement, Analytics, and Cost Engineering). We help these studios understand their impact by running experiments, defining and building performance metrics, and delivering analyses that guide decision-making. As a Data Scientist on the team, you’ll collaborate across disciplines to surface insights, shape product thinking, and strengthen a culture of evidence-driven decisions.

title: Director of Engineering, Experience
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.

Delivering the best Spotify experience possible. To as many people as possible. In as many moments as possible. That’s what the Experience Mission is all about. We use our deep understanding of consumer expectations to enrich the lives of millions of users around the world, bringing the music, audio, and video they love to the devices, apps, and platforms they use every day.

This position sits at the heart of Spotify’s Content Platform, responsible for the systems that define, structure, and govern all content across the platform. Our mission is to provide a trusted, consistent source of truth so content can be reliably managed and reused across every Spotify experience. We build and operate the core systems and workflows that ensure content is accurate, complete, and available; from APIs and life cycle orchestration through to content quality and integrity.

As Spotify’s content ecosystem continues to grow in scale and complexity — across new formats, use cases, and regulatory environments — this domain plays a critical role in ensuring we scale intentionally while maintaining the trust and quality our users and creators expect.

We’re now seeking a Director of Engineering to lead this domain — shaping its technical direction, developing teams, and evolving the systems that underpin Spotify’s global content platform.

title: Director of Sales - Backstage
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

Backstage was created at Spotify and has grown into a leading SaaS and open source platform for developer portals. Thousands of companies use Backstage to bring structure to complex microservices environments, improve developer experience, and ship high-quality software faster — including in the age of AI — without losing autonomy or control.

This role leads sales for Shipping Our Expertise (SOE), Spotify's B2B SaaS business built on our platform capabilities. Our flagship products — Backstage Portal and Confidence — are AI-first and built to support both human developers and automated systems. Everything we bring to market is battle-tested at Spotify scale, giving our customers confidence that our solutions work in the most complex R&D environments.

title: Director of Sales, Emerging & Scaled (Southern Europe)
description:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 

We are seeking an experienced Director of Sales to join our Emerging & Scaled business team (E&S). The E&S team focuses on serving mid and small-sized and news businesses with our advertising solutions, helping them reach their audience and achieve their marketing goals on the Spotify platform.

As a Director of Sales for the E&S business, you will play a pivotal role in developing and expanding our presence across Southern Europe (France, Italy, Spain). You will lead a team of Client Partners, working closely with them to drive growth, build client relationships, and deliver exceptional results. Reporting to the Head of E&S Europe, you will be responsible for crafting and implementing a strategic go-to-market plan tailored to the unique needs of E&S clients across Southern Europe, with a particular focus on building and scaling our New Business motion in the region.

We want someone who loves our product, knows the Southern European ad market, clients and agency landscape, can build creative, long-term consultative solutions, and lead, motivate, operationalize, and grow a sales team. You should be confident without being arrogant, and hardworking while still knowing how to have fun.
department: Advertising Sales - Emerging and Scaled Sales

title: Editor Music and Culture, International & Hip-Hop
description:
The Music Mission team owns Spotify’s end to end proposition for music creators and the experiences they create for fans. The team is dedicated to building tools and services to enable creation, promotion, expression, and monetization at scale.

You’ll join Spotify’s Editorial team in India, where music, culture, and storytelling come together to shape how millions of listeners discover and connect with music every day. As part of a global network of editors, you’ll help define how international music and hip-hop show up for audiences in India—and how Indian hip-hop travels beyond borders.

title: Engineering Manager - AI Fleet Management & Honk
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

What if the systems you built changed how millions of developers work — not just at Spotify, but across the industry? The Platform Developer Experience (PDX) team builds the infrastructure and internal products that power how Spotify engineers ship software. Within PDX, the Wingspan team is building the next generation of fleet management and reimagining how we interact with AI agents — and bringing those capabilities to market through Portal, Spotify’s external developer platform built on Backstage.
 
We are redesigning platform engineering around intelligent agents, large language models, and decision-aware automation. In this role, you’ll lead a team shaping how AI becomes a core operating layer of modern platform infrastructure, directly improving how developers ship and maintain software at scale.

title: Engineering Manager - Experimentation
description:
Think it, build it, ship it, tweak it" is at the core of Spotify's product development approach. In the Experimentation product area we are providing Spotify and external customers with Confidence, our platform to quickly and safely iterate through ideas, learn from them and make informed decisions for better products.
 
At Spotify, experimentation touches almost all of our functions from marketing via content management to consumer products, and we’re running several thousands of tests every year. Through confidence, we 're sharing all our findings and practices with more companies as a commercial SaaS product.
 
The team you will manage is a skilled and highly engaged group of engineers that are distributed over Europe. As an Engineering manager, you coach them individually and as a team, maintaining their strong team culture and helping them thrive and and grow. Your technical experience provides mentorship and guidelines to the team, which ensures quality and timeliness of your deliveries. Your leadership promotes creativity, collaboration and courage, which forms a kind and productive team with a high bar for quality and pace of delivery.

title: Engineering Manager - Social Listening
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.

We’re building one of Spotify’s most social and engaging experiences: Jam. Jam brings people together through music, enabling friends and groups to listen in real time, wherever they are. It transforms listening from a solo activity into a shared experience and plays an important role in how Spotify connects users through audio.

You’ll join a cross-functional team working across mobile, backend, and platform technologies to shape how millions of people experience music together.

title: Experiential Marketing Manager
description: At Spotify, we connect millions of fans to the music and creators they love. Our marketing team brings that connection to life through culture-first storytelling, bold creativity, and experiences that resonate far beyond the app. This role sits within the Marketing team, working at the intersection of music, culture, and fandom to deliver unforgettable experiences across India.

title: Experiential Marketing Manager, South Korea
description:
At Spotify, we don’t just build marketing campaigns—we create moments that bring fans and artists closer together. Within our K-pop Marketing team, you’ll help shape how K-pop is experienced both on and off the platform. This team sits at the intersection of culture, creativity, and fandom, driving impactful campaigns and live experiences that resonate locally in Korea while influencing global audiences.

Your mission is to bridge the gap between physical live experiences and digital streaming. You will lead the strategy and execution of high-impact activations—ranging from album launches and exclusive artist performances to Spotify’s original programmed events. By straddling the line between innovative concepting and rigorous execution, you will amplify our brand and drive growth for the company.

Based in our Seoul office, you will be a key architect in strengthening the bond between artists and fans, ensuring every activation resonates locally while scaling globally.

title: Financial Systems Analyst - Platform
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

The Oracles sit at the heart of Spotify’s financial infrastructure, building and evolving the systems that power our subledger and accounting lifecycle. From accruals to reconciliation and ledger integrations, the team is driving a broader FinE initiative to standardize and scale how accounting flows across Spotify. You’ll work closely with engineers, finance partners, and product teams to bring clarity, speed, and accuracy to complex financial systems.

title: Frontend Engineer - Music
description:
The Rights Systems team builds and operates Spotify Rights Center (SRC), Spotify’s rights management platform that enables the company and its partners to identify, manage, and enforce music licensing rights across creator- and artist-generated content.

Comparable in scope to YouTube’s Content ID, SRC is designed specifically for Spotify’s ecosystem and plays an important role in supporting new formats like video and other emerging content types. Over the past year the team launched SRC from inception to a fully operational production platform, now live and used by real rightsholders.

The platform includes automated content scanning, policy management, enforcement pipelines, dispute workflows, and analytics. The team has grown from a single squad to three squads (approximately 29 engineers, designers, and product specialists) and continues to scale as a strategic investment area for Spotify over the next several years.

title: Full Stack Engineer - Audiobooks
description:
Spotify for Authors (SFA) is the creator platform for the audiobook industry — a web application that gives publishers, authors, and narrators tools to manage their catalog, track   performance, and distribute content on Spotify. We're scaling toward 100,000 users across a React/TypeScript frontend, Java backend services, and data pipelines that feed into Spotify's   broader audiobook ecosystem.

SFA connects to Spotify's highest-priority bets: expanding audiobook access, building content creation tools, and shaping discovery surfaces. We operate with a "short toes" philosophy —   contribution has no borders, quality is shared, and we use modern tools (including AI) to reduce toil and amplify creativity.

title: Group Product Manager - Messaging
description:
Messaging is a business-critical system at Spotify. It sits between many message senders and a shared pool of users across several channels (e.g. in-app, push, email, and emerging channels), delivering more than 70 billion impressions per year.

The Messaging team operates within the Freemium Funnel Area, where product, engineering, design, insights, and marketing collaborate to shape how users engage with Spotify across monetization experiences. We are responsible for audience reach, messaging governance, campaign delivery, and the platforms that enable Spotify teams to communicate with users at scale — reliably, responsibly, and effectively.

We have ambitious plans to evolve Messaging from a delivery layer into an intelligent, governed platform that reasons about what to send, to whom, and when — balancing user experience with business outcomes. We are looking for a Group Product Manager to help lead this transformation.

title: Head of Backstage Marketing
description:

title: Head of Government Affairs Australia New Zealand
description:
You will join Spotify’s Global Government Affairs team, working at the intersection of policy, technology, and creativity. This team partners with governments, regulators, and industry groups around the world to help shape a fair, innovative, and inclusive digital ecosystem.

In Australia and New Zealand, you’ll play a key role in representing Spotify’s voice on issues that matter to creators, listeners, and the broader media landscape—ranging from competition and online safety to AI and the future of the creative economy.

title: Lead Growth Manager- UK/IE/NL
description:
You’ll join the Europe Growth team within the Markets & Subscriptions organisation, focused on bringing Spotify to more listeners across the region. This team sits at the intersection of marketing, product, data, and commercial strategy, working closely with local market teams to grow both free and Premium users.

In this role, you’ll own growth across the UK, Ireland, and Netherlands—shaping strategy, running experiments, and turning insights into action that drives meaningful impact.

title: Lead Music Development, Türkiye
description: The Lead, Music Development acts as the principal architect in fortifying Spotify’s relationships and standing within the Turkish music landscape across labels, industry bodies, and other institutional stakeholders. Acting as the spokesperson, this role strengthens Spotify’s position in the Turkish music ecosystem by shaping clear narratives and leading strategic initiatives and events. You will address industry sentiment, focus on strengthening institutional relationships, and connect with internal global music leadership to ensure a seamless flow of information and feedback.

title: Lead, User Growth Optimization
description:
We are seeking a Lead, User Growth Optimization to elevate our conversion optimization strategy. This role involves both implementing current optimization initiatives and developing future conversion strategies and hypotheses. The successful candidate will report to the User Growth Optimization Director within the Growth team, focusing on increasing subscriptions, reducing churn, and maximizing lifetime value.

Join the world’s fastest-growing online subscription business in a high-impact role where you will put a personal mark on the success of Spotify by delivering even faster subscriber growth.

title: Legal Counsel - Commerce & Subscriptions
description: You’ll join Spotify’s Commerce & Subscriptions Legal team, part of the broader Products and Services Legal team, supporting a fast-moving part of the business focused on how users purchase, manage, and experience paid offerings. This team partners closely with product, engineering, design, and business stakeholders to enable innovative e-commerce experiences while managing legal risk in a thoughtful, pragmatic way.

title: Legal Counsel - Licensing & Business Development
description: You’ll join Spotify’s Legal Licensing and Business Development team, supporting partnerships that bring Spotify to listeners around the world. This team works at the intersection of technology, media, and business, helping shape how Spotify is distributed across platforms, devices, and experiences. You’ll collaborate closely with business development, finance, and licensing teams to support innovative deals that expand Spotify’s global reach.

title: Legal Counsel - Platform Liability
description:
Spotify’s Legal team is growing. We’re facing innovative challenges that mirror the ground-breaking industry and product at the heart of our company. As we’ve expanded into more than 184 countries and our company has grown tremendously, we find ourselves facing new and interesting legal issues every day.

As Legal Counsel, Platform Liability & Enforcement at Spotify, you will help supervise Spotify’s legal takedown practices and policies and you will provide legal support for the teams that develop, implement, and support Spotify’s content policies. You will also work directly to resolve claims related to these policies and help build out processes for scale. Your experience making risk-based assessments and proactively finding solutions to complex problems will be crucial.

title: Manager, Partner Product and Delivery
description: We are looking for a Manager, Partner Product and Delivery, who will join our team spread across the globe, and help manage Spotify's important Premium Partnerships projects in Latin America. You will have the operational and day-to-day responsibility to manage the technical implementation and performance of the partnerships. You will report directly to the Associate Director, Partner Product and Delivery.

title: Manager, Production - Brazil
description:
Spotify’s Marketing team in Latin America helps shape how Spotify shows up across the region through culture, creativity, and storytelling. We create work that feels authentic, locally relevant, and connected to the artists, creators, and fans who make Spotify unique.

Based in São Paulo, and working closely with teams across Latin America, this role will lead production for brand initiatives in Brazil, ensuring creative ideas are delivered with excellence from concept through execution. You’ll combine strong creative judgment with operational expertise to bring impactful work to life while balancing quality, timelines, and budgets.

title: Manager, Production - Mexico
description:
Spotify’s Marketing team in Latin America helps shape how Spotify shows up across the region through culture, creativity, and storytelling. We create work that feels authentic, locally relevant, and connected to the artists, creators, and fans who make Spotify unique.

Based in Mexico City and working closely with teams across Latin America, this role will lead production for brand initiatives in Mexico, ensuring creative ideas are delivered with excellence from concept through execution. You’ll combine strong creative judgment with operational expertise to bring impactful work to life while balancing quality, timelines, and budgets.

title: Manager, Technical Advertising Solutions - EMEA
description:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 

We're looking for a Manager, Technical Advertising Solutions to lead a team responsible for ensuring the successful delivery of advertising campaigns across Spotify's Ads business. You'll partner closely with Sales, Product, Engineering, buyers, and DSP partners to solve complex technical challenges across our Programmatic and Direct advertising products, improve campaign performance, and help scale a world-class advertising platform.

The Manager, Technical Advertising Solutions will serve as the operational and technical lead for their regional team — driving quality, optimizing campaign delivery, and fostering strong partnerships with internal and external stakeholders. This role works in close coordination with our offshore support teams and reports to the Head of Global Advertising Solutions (Programmatic & Direct).
department: Advertising Sales - Emerging and Scaled Sales

title: Manager, Technical Advertising Solutions APAC
description:
We're looking for a Manager, Technical Advertising Solutions to lead a team responsible for ensuring the successful delivery of advertising campaigns across Spotify's Ads business. You'll partner closely with Sales, Product, Engineering, buyers, and DSP partners to solve complex technical challenges across our Programmatic and Direct advertising products, improve campaign performance, and help scale a world-class advertising platform.

The Manager, Technical Advertising Solutions will serve as the operational and technical lead for their regional team — driving quality, optimizing campaign delivery, and fostering strong partnerships with internal and external stakeholders. This role works in close coordination with our offshore support teams and reports to the Head of Global Advertising Solutions (Programmatic & Direct).
department: Advertising Sales - Emerging and Scaled Sales

title: Marketing Manager UK/IE
description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an experienced Marketing Manager to join the team at a time of significant strategic ambition. Working as one with teams across the business, to plan, and deliver high-impact marketing campaigns across culture, music, and content, helping fans connect deeper to the artists and creators they love both on and off the platform.

This role requires expertise in creative campaign ideation, end-to-end XFN campaign leadership, live event and content production, multi-channel marketing, and the ability to translate cultural moments and releases into innovative and impactful fan experiences and content.

title: Marketing Manager, Brand Experience
description:
About the team
You’ll join Spotify’s Central Europe Marketing team, part of our Markets organization, focused on building meaningful connections with audiences across Germany, Austria, and Switzerland for 1 year fixed term in Berlin!  This team brings Spotify to life through culturally relevant campaigns, brand activations, and live experiences that resonate deeply with local music and youth culture. You’ll collaborate across global and regional teams to shape how Spotify shows up in culture across the DACH region.

title: Music Editor, Türkiye
description:
The Music Mission team owns Spotify’s end to end proposition for music creators and the experiences they create for fans. The team is dedicated to building tools and services to enable creation, promotion, expression, and monetization at scale.

In Spotify, the Editorial team shapes how millions of listeners discover and connect with music every day. In this role, you’ll help define Spotify’s editorial strategy for Türkiye—bringing together deep cultural knowledge and data-informed programming to create playlists and experiences that feel timely, relevant, and distinctly local.

Currently the team is looking for a passionate and knowledgeable music professional to join our team in Dubai. If you have an expert ear for music, a deep understanding of the Turkish culture and music ecosystem, and a strong ability to blend data-driven insights with cultural expertise, we’d love to hear from you.

In this role, you’ll curate and shape the music listening experience, programming playlists across moods, moments, and genres. You’ll play a key role in developing our editorial strategy for Türkiye while keeping a pulse on emerging trends, rising artists, and cultural moments. Your work will directly impact how audiences engage with music on Spotify.

title: Partner Marketing Manager
description:
Spotify is looking for a creative, commercially minded, and highly motivated Partner Marketing Manager to join our India Marketing team. In this role, you will develop and execute innovative marketing partnerships that help drive revenue growth, strengthen Spotify’s cultural relevance, and create meaningful value for both Spotify and our partners.

You will identify and build relationships with brands that align with Spotify’s strategic priorities across Premium, Brand, Product, and Music. Working across multiple functions, you will develop compelling partnership concepts and oversee their execution from idea through launch and measurement. This is a highly collaborative role that sits within the Marketing team and operates closely with Ad Sales to unlock new growth opportunities for Spotify India.

title: Podcast Manager, India
description: Spotify’s Podcast team in India is focused on growing and evolving one of the world’s most dynamic creator ecosystems. We work closely with creators, podcast networks, agencies, and internal global teams to shape how podcast and video creators grow their audiences and build sustainable communities on Spotify. This role sits at the intersection of creator partnerships, culture, content strategy, and audience growth.

title: Principal Product Manager - ML / Subscriptions Growth
description:
Spotify’s mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators.
 
The Subscriptions Mission builds and evolves Spotify’s subscription products and marketplace experiences to drive sustainable user and revenue growth globally. We focus on awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization strategies that help users unlock the full value of Spotify while enabling the business to scale efficiently and responsibly.
 
User Understanding sits within Subscriptions and focuses on building the intelligence layer that makes our growth efforts smarter—through decisioning, data signals, and ML models that power personalization across surfaces and lifecycle moments. You’ll work at the intersection of machine learning innovation and commercial strategy, partnering closely with ML engineers, data scientists, data platform teams, product insights, and business stakeholders to shape high-impact growth initiatives.

title: Product Designer - Core Experience (Core Value & Engagement)
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.
 
We’re looking for mid-level to senior product designers with strong visual design skills to join the team focusing on the core mobile experience. This team works on some of the most essential parts of the Spotify experience that shapes some of Spotify’s most iconic and emotionally resonant experiences - the features that help people connect with music, organize their libraries, and engage more deeply with what they love. You might be designing how users share and discover music with friends, how they create customise and manage playlists, or Whether it’s evolving how users organize their music, creating new ways to share audio socially, or integrating AI to help users find their next favorite song, you will work on the products that define the Core Spotify experience.
 
It’s a high-impact, fast-moving space where priorities evolve as we explore new ways to bring users closer to the content they care about. As a designer here, you’ll have the opportunity to shape foundational experiences that millions of people use every day.This is a role for a designer who thrives on variety, moves quickly, and wants to see their work impact millions of listeners across multiple surfaces

title: Research Scientist - Music
description:
We are seeking a Research Scientist to join our Artist-First AI Music lab. Our team pioneers and advances state-of-the-art generative technologies for music that create breakthrough experiences for fans and artists. We invent entirely new listening experiences that center and celebrate artists and creatives. All of our products will put artists and songwriters first, through these four principles:
 
Partnerships with record labels, distributors, and music publishers: We’ll develop new products for artists and fans through upfront agreements, not by asking for forgiveness later.
Choice in participation: We recognize there’s a wide range of views on use of generative music tools within the artistic community. Therefore, artists and rightsholders will choose if and how to participate to ensure the use of AI tools aligns with the values of the people behind the music.
Fair compensation and new revenue: We will build products that create wholly new revenue streams for rightsholders, artists, and songwriters, ensuring they are properly compensated for uses of their work and transparently credited for their contributions.
Artist-fan connection: AI tools we develop will not replace human artistry. They will give artists new ways to be creative and connect with fans. We will leverage our role as the place where more than 700 million people already come to listen to music every month to ensure that generative AI deepens artist-fan connections.
 
For more information, see this press release: https://newsroom.spotify.com/2025-10-16/artist-first-ai-music-spotify-collaboration/

title: Sales Trainer, LATAM
description:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts. 

Our Ad Sales Training & Enablement team helps Spotify’s global Sales organization perform at its best. We design and deliver learning experiences that keep our sellers confident, informed, and customer-focused. Working closely with Sales Leadership, Product Marketing, and Business Marketing, we build programs that support growth across Spotify’s evolving advertising business.

title: Security Engineer- Product Security
description:
Security engineers at Spotify help protect the security of our platform and the experience of more than 700 million users around the world. We're looking for an experienced engineer to join us in securing some of Spotify’s most important engineering initiatives.

You’ll work across product and infrastructure security, partnering with autonomous development and platform teams to build secure systems at scale. We’re a distributed team that combines deep security expertise with practical tooling and guidance, helping engineers move quickly while continuously strengthening Spotify’s security posture. We value curiosity, collaboration, and a willingness to both teach and learn from others.

You bring experience in security, software, or systems engineering and enjoy solving complex technical challenges. In this role, you’ll represent security across a variety of engineering and business contexts, so you’re comfortable communicating with diverse audiences and translating security concepts into practical outcomes.

title: Senior Accountant - APAC & Japan
description:
The APAC Accounting Hub in Singapore plays a critical role in ensuring accurate financial reporting, strong compliance, and smooth coordination across markets. As part of Spotify’s global Finance team, we support a fast-growing, cross-border business that operates with speed, accountability, and high standards.
 
We’re looking for a highly self-motivated Senior Accountant to serve as the day-to-day Japan finance point of contact while supporting broader APAC reporting. This role is ideal for someone who is hands-on, structured, and comfortable operating in a lean, fast-paced environment with cross-border complexity. You’ll help embed Japanese language capability within the APAC team and partner with regional and global stakeholders across Finance and other functions as needed.

title: Senior Applied Research Engineer - Media Quality
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.

We are looking for a Senior Applied Research Engineer who brings deep research expertise and strong engineering skills across both audio and video. In this role, you will help advance how Spotify understands, evaluates, and improves media experiences across platforms. You’ll work across the full media pipeline—from backend processing such as ingestion, encoding, and delivery to frontend playback, buffering, and synchronization—solving product problems with research discipline: structured experiments, solid metrics, and evidence-based decisions, in a real production environment.

You’ll join a team working at the intersection of media quality, perception, and engineering. The team builds applied solutions that improve how audio and video experiences perform across Spotify. By turning deep technical understanding into scalable capabilities, they help ensure a consistent, high-quality experience for hundreds of millions of users across devices and platforms.

title: Senior Client Engineer - Core Experience (Formats & Foundations)
description:

Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.

Formats & Foundations is part of Spotify’s Core Experience Studio, focused on helping users connect more deeply with creators and their content. The team owns and evolves some of Spotify’s most highly trafficked experiences, including Artist, Album, Podcast, Episode, and Audiobook surfaces.

Beyond building user-facing experiences, the team develops foundational mobile capabilities, reusable components, and platform infrastructure that enable teams across Spotify to deliver high-quality, consistent experiences at scale. We work at the intersection of product experience and platform engineering, balancing innovation, performance, and usability across the Spotify app.

We’re looking for a Senior Client Engineer who is passionate about mobile craftsmanship, scalable architecture, and building tools and foundations that empower product teams. You’ll help shape technical direction, mentor engineers, and contribute to experiences used by millions of listeners every day.

title: Senior Editor, Music Editorial - Nordics
description:
We are looking for an experienced Senior Editor for Norway & Finland, to join Spotify’s Music Editorial team based in Stockholm. In this role you will be a part of the Music team in the Nordics, reporting into the regional Head of Editorial.

You will contribute your expertise to various music-related projects within the company, including playlist curation, creative editorial work, and cross-functional collaboration with different teams in the region and beyond. You will provide deep cultural and musical insights to create highly engaging and cohesive music experiences for listeners in Norway and Finland, shaping how listeners in these markets experience music on Spotify across playlists, programming, and emerging editorial formats. Additionally, you will drive artist discovery through an extensive editorial ecosystem of playlists and tools, critically assessing music trends and user behaviour to identify opportunities and emerging trends.

You will demonstrate a team player spirit, a passion for performance-oriented analytics, and a solid understanding of Spotify’s role in the music industry both locally and globally. Additionally, you will have an editorial personality that thrives in engaging audiences through emerging, creative Editorial formats, bringing music to life in innovative ways.

As a music editor, you will be encouraged to make data-informed decisions while also contributing creative and cultural expertise. Above all, your work will impact the way the world experiences music!

title: Senior Engineer- Mobile iOS Platform
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

At Spotify, we’re on a mission to unlock the potential of human creativity by connecting millions of listeners with the creators they love. Within the Client Platform Studio, the Synchrotron squad builds the architectural foundation that powers our mobile experiences.

As our mobile codebase continues to grow, we’re focused on keeping development efficient, scalable, and enjoyable for engineers across Spotify. This team enables developers to move fast by reducing complexity, improving system boundaries, and creating clear, reliable building blocks for application development.

title: Senior Full Stack Engineer - WhoSampled
description:
Every great song has a story behind it: a sample flipped into something new, a melody borrowed and reborn, a classic reinterpreted for a new generation. WhoSampled is where those stories live.

WhoSampled is the world's leading database of music samples, interpolations, cover songs, and remixes. Built over more than a decade by a passionate community of over 40,000 contributors, and verified by a dedicated team of moderators, it's become the definitive archive of music's creative lineage — visited by millions of fans each year. In late 2025, WhoSampled joined Spotify, powering SongDNA: a new experience that lets listeners explore the samples, covers, and interpolations woven into the music they love.

We're a small team with an outsized impact, and we're growing. As a Senior Fullstack Engineer, you'll be one of the first engineering hires into this team — a genuine opportunity to shape how WhoSampled evolves as a standalone product and as a foundational layer within Spotify's ecosystem.

As a small team, our work spans across the stack: you’ll work as much on data pipelines and backend APIs as you will on our web app. The ideal candidate is a generalist at heart — someone who's equally at home writing a Django view, tuning a PostgreSQL query, or shipping a UI improvement, and someone who gets results without waiting for someone else to own adjacent layers.

title: Senior Fullstack Engineer - Commerce Platform
description:
The Subscriptions Mission builds and evolves the experiences and systems that allow millions of listeners to discover, subscribe to, and enjoy Spotify Premium. Our teams focus on awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and commerce — ensuring seamless experiences that help fans connect more deeply with the audio they love while enabling sustainable growth for Spotify.

The Commerce Platform powers the purchasing and subscription systems that enable millions of users worldwide to access Spotify Premium. Within this area, the Payment SDK squad is responsible for the technology that handles initial payment transactions across our platform.

As a Senior Fullstack Engineer, you’ll work on systems that process large volumes of transactions and support the global purchase experience. Your work will help evolve the platform that powers Spotify’s commerce ecosystem, ensuring our payment infrastructure remains scalable, reliable, and innovative as we grow.

title: Senior Fullstack Engineer – Generative UI Platform
description:
UI Studio is Spotify’s internal generative UI prototyping platform that enables teams to spin up fully functioning replicas of Spotify surfaces — complete with playable music — to explore and pitch new product ideas. What started as a small internal project has evolved into a widely adopted platform, and we’re now expanding it into a full generative UI system where teams can prompt for the interfaces they need.

As part of Spotify’s Platform mission, we build the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily — creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to support our global business and millions of creators and fans.

We’re looking for a Senior Fullstack Engineer who is genuinely fullstack — strong across backend systems and confident building high-quality frontend experiences — to help scale the platform’s architecture and AI-native capabilities. You’ll shape core technical decisions, lead complex initiatives end-to-end, and elevate engineering standards across the team.

title: Senior HR Generalist
description:
We are looking for a Senior HR Generalist to join our Global HR team, based in Singapore and overseeing our markets in JAPAC. Working closely with the HRBP for SAMEA & JAPAC, this role is both strategic and hands-on, partnering with business leaders to shape our people strategy locally while ensuring a high-quality, consistent employee experience throughout the employee lifecycle.
 
You will play a key role in helping our business navigate growth, organizational change, workforce planning, and people development. You’ll support leaders and teams through our core HR processes while keeping us aligned with global ways of working and compliant with local regulations. As part of a global HR community, you will collaborate with HR Business Partners, Employment Legal, Talent Acquisition, Compensation & Benefits, Learning & Development, Diversity & Inclusion, and other partners to deliver a world-class people experience.

title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer - Content Intelligence
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.

The Verbatim squad sits within the Enrichment & Content Intelligence product area and is focused on helping Spotify better understand audio, text, and visual content through machine learning. The team develops technologies that power experiences across Spotify including content skipping, transcription, moderation, and visual understanding. Working at the intersection of large-scale machine learning and product innovation, the squad partners closely with Product, Engineering, and Data Science teams to build intelligent systems that improve how users experience content across the platform.

title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer - Generative UI Platform
description:
UI Studio is Spotify’s internal generative UI prototyping platform that enables teams to spin up fully functioning replicas of Spotify surfaces — complete with playable music — to explore and pitch new product ideas. What started as a small internal project has evolved into a widely adopted platform, and we’re now expanding it into a full generative UI system where teams can prompt for the interfaces they need.

You’ll join a small, high-impact team working at the intersection of machine learning, design systems, and product development — helping shape how user interfaces are imagined and built at Spotify.

title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer - Messaging Platform
description:
Spotify’s Subscriptions Mission focuses on converting listeners into lifelong subscribers by delivering seamless, valuable experiences across pricing, packaging, and customer journeys. We build the systems and tools that power acquisition, retention, and overall subscription growth at scale.

The Messaging Platform powers Spotify’s communications to over a billion users — from push notifications to emails and in-app messages that connect listeners to the content they love. Within this space, the Paloma squad focuses on message optimization: deciding which message reaches which user, through which channel, and at what moment.

We’re evolving how messaging works at Spotify — moving from short-term optimization toward systems that understand long-term user journeys. By combining reinforcement learning approaches with deeper domain signals, we’re expanding how machine learning shapes the entire messaging funnel.

title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer - Policy & Safety
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.


The Policy & Safety team sits within Content Platform in the Experience Mission, building the systems that keep Spotify safe, compliant, and trusted by millions of users and creators. This team owns Spotify’s content moderation infrastructure — from detection models to policy enforcement systems and compliance data pipelines.

Working at the intersection of machine learning, platform engineering, and regulatory compliance, the team partners closely with Trust & Safety, Legal, and Public Affairs. They’re on the critical path for every new content type and social feature — including messaging, comments, and collaborative experiences — ensuring safety is built in from day one. With a strong focus on “safety by default,” the team is investing in large-scale rearchitecture and ML-driven systems to proactively protect users and empower safer interactions across the platform.

title: Senior Manager, EU Government Affairs
description:
We are seeking a dedicated and dynamic candidate to join Spotify’s EU Government Affairs team in Brussels as Senior Manager - EU Government Affairs. 

The Government Affairs team is charged with understanding the global and local policy landscape in relation to Spotify’s business objectives and advocating on the company’s behalf on critical public policy issues. We represent the company before legislators and regulators, interact with civil society and industry, see around corners, and mitigate potential risks.

In this role you will be an active member and will provide advice and external advocacy on policy matters that have an impact on Spotify in Europe. You will report to our Senior Director of European Policy and will be based in our Brussels office.

title: Senior Product Manager - ML / Subscriptions Growth
description:

You’ll work at the intersection of machine learning innovation and commercial strategy, partnering closely with ML engineers, data scientists, data platform teams, product insights, and business stakeholders to shape high-impact growth initiatives.

title: Senior Product Manager - Sharing & Social
description:
In the Experience Mission, we design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. On this team, our mission is to show up wherever people are being social — across the platforms, surfaces, and moments where they're already expressing themselves and connecting with others — and make it effortless to bring audio into those moments. That means being present in the feeds, messages, and communities where culture moves, giving listeners the tools to express who they are through what they hear, and creating the loops that turn shared content into a new connection, a new fan, or a new Spotify user.

About the team

The team sits within PPX and owns Spotify's sharing surfaces and off-platform social presence: from the core share sheet to partner integrations, social formats, and the growth loops that bring new listeners in. You'll work closely with platform partners, business development, feature-owning teams and cross-functional engineering and design teams. In this role, you’ll shape how Spotify shows up across the social platforms of today and the future. You’ll be the face of product across our social strategic partners, while owning end-to-end product execution.

title: Senior Product Manager - Subscriptions Mission
description:
Mission Statement
The Subscriptions Mission builds and evolves Spotify's subscription products and marketplace experiences to drive sustainable user and revenue growth globally. We focus on awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization strategies that help users unlock the full value of Spotify while enabling the business to scale efficiently and responsibly.

About the Team
This role sits at the heart of the Subscriptions Mission and owns how users move through the freemium funnel — from the moment a free user encounters Premium value, through trial, conversion, activation, and into long-term retention. You’ll partner closely with product, design, marketing, finance, pricing & packaging, and regional commercial teams to shape the end-to-end subscriber journey across plans and markets.

title: Senior Product Manager – Financial Engineering - Tax Solutions
description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

At Spotify, our Financial Engineering teams build and scale the internal systems that power global financial operations across a rapidly evolving business. Within the FinE Alliance, you’ll work closely with Tax, Treasury, Accounting, and Engineering partners to shape products that enable operational excellence, compliance, and automation at global scale. This role sits at the intersection of finance, product strategy, enterprise systems, and engineering, helping Spotify continuously improve how financial processes operate across regions and regulatory environments.

title: Senior Product Manager – Partner Growth
description:
Delivering the best Spotify experience, everywhere and every time. As AI assistants, widgets, and mobile surfaces evolve, Spotify must meet users wherever they are. Partner Growth leads this work, building Spotify’s AI-driven experiences with major partners like Anthropic, Meta, OpenAI, Google, and Samsung, and delivering high-impact mobile ubiquity features such as homescreen widgets, lockscreen surfaces, and navigation integrations.

As Senior Product Manager for Partner Growth, you will shape Spotify’s experience layer across partner ecosystems and mobile platforms. You'll define the product strategy for some of our most important partnerships, advocate for the right outcomes across the organization, and take hands-on accountability for delivering the features that bring that strategy to life. This role blends strategic partnership leadership with end-to-end product execution.

title: Senior Product Manager, Core Experience (Playback)
description:

Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. We’re seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the playback experience across Spotify’s mobile apps. This is one of the most highly used parts of the product, where millions of users engage with music, podcasts, audiobooks, and video every day. You’ll shape how content, controls, and context come together to create seamless, personalized listening experiences in real time.

title: Senior Security Engineer - Enterprise Security
description:
We're hiring a Senior Security Engineer to lead and raise the bar for enterprise security at Spotify. In this role, you'll be hands-on across core security domains, IAM, endpoint management, networking, and shaping how we secure an increasingly AI-enabled enterprise. You'll build tooling and automation, define policies and guardrails, and partner closely with teams across IT, infrastructure, legal, and beyond to keep Spotify moving fast without compromising on security.

The ideal candidate brings solid professional experience in enterprise security, strong technical foundations, and a genuine comfort with AI, both as a tool to augment their own work and as a surface area to secure. Just as importantly, you're an excellent communicator who can translate security priorities into language that resonates across a global, cross-functional organization.

title: Senior Software Engineer - TV Playback (C++)
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.
 
At Spotify, we’re building the best way to experience audio and video — everywhere, every time. As listening and watching on TV continues to grow, we’re investing deeply in native, video-first playback experiences that are fast, reliable, and feel effortless.
 
The Native Playback team sits at the heart of Spotify’s TV strategy. We integrate Spotify’s playback experience with the native players and media frameworks available on each platform. Our work happens close to the system — where performance, reliability, and quality truly matter — and directly impacts how millions of people experience Spotify every day.

title: Senior Staff Machine Learning Engineer - Content Policy & Safety
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.

The Content Platform team powers the full lifecycle of content across music, podcasts, audiobooks, and emerging formats at Spotify. We ensure that everything from licensed catalog to user-generated content is trusted, safe, and high quality for millions of listeners worldwide. Our systems are responsible for how content is ingested, understood, enriched, governed, and distributed across the platform. As the scale and diversity of content continues to grow, driven by advances in AI and new creation tools—we’re investing in systems that ensure content remains safe, compliant, and high quality.

We’re seeking a Senior Staff Machine Learning Engineer to build and scale ML systems that power safety, policy enforcement, and compliance across Spotify. In this role, you’ll shape how automated systems evaluate and act on content—ensuring decisions are consistent, explainable, and reliable at global scale. This work is critical to maintaining trust for both listeners and creators.

title: Senior Staff Machine Learning Engineer, Content Platform
description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify. 

The Content Platform team powers the full lifecycle of content across music, podcasts, audiobooks, and emerging formats at Spotify. We ensure that everything from licensed catalog to user-generated content is trusted, safe, and high quality for millions of listeners worldwide. Our systems are responsible for how content is ingested, understood, enriched, governed, and distributed across the platform. As the scale and diversity of content continues to grow—driven by advances in AI and new creation tools—we’re building intelligent systems that can evaluate, manage, and route content reliably at global scale.

We’re seeking a Senior Staff Machine Learning Engineer to build and scale foundational ML systems that power content understanding, safety, and decisioning across the platform. In this role, you’ll shape the architecture and technical strategy that ensures content is evaluated, governed, and safely delivered at global scale. This work is foundational to delivering safe, high-quality experiences for both listeners and creators, while enabling new ways to interact with content across Spotify.

title: Social Marketing Manager
description:
Spotify is looking for an exceptional Social Marketing Manager to lead our social marketing efforts across the UK and Ireland, while providing strategic oversight and guidance for the Netherlands market. We're seeking a social-first marketer with a deep passion for music culture, audience behaviour, and storytelling — someone who can think strategically while also driving day-to-day execution across channels.

You bring a proven track record of developing impactful social strategies, creating culturally resonant content, and building engaged communities. You understand platform dynamics, know how to identify opportunities within emerging trends, and can translate audience insights into compelling campaigns that drive engagement, growth, and brand affinity.

In this role, you'll collaborate across Marketing, Music, Podcasts, Audiobooks, PR & Communications, Artist Partnerships, and local market teams to bring Spotify to life for fans across multiple markets. You'll help position Spotify at the center of culture, connecting audiences with the artists, creators, and experiences they love.

title: Staff Engineer - Content Intelligence Infrastructure

The Content Intelligence Product Area is a core part of Spotify’s Content Platform within the Experience mission. We build systems that create deep, machine-readable understanding of content across audio, video, text, and images before it reaches users. This enables automation, safety, and entirely new product experiences. Our work powers content enrichment, quality analysis, annotation platforms, and scalable intelligence systems that allow teams across Spotify to build without needing direct content understanding expertise.

We’re now looking for a Staff Engineer to help build and scale foundational infrastructure powering content understanding across Spotify. This role sits at the intersection of backend systems, ML infrastructure, distributed data systems, and AI-enabled platform capabilities. You’ll help shape infrastructure strategy across the product area while partnering with senior engineers, product leaders, and cross-functional teams to execute on long-term technical investments.
